channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-06-25
item.description:
Our open-source research community has seen exciting progress across multiple projects. In the `scienceverse/metacheck` project, the `dev` branch received significant updates, including fixes and additions to ensure clean development installations and improvements to various checks. Meanwhile, `giladfeldman/docpluck` saw documentation updates and fixes to address issues with tables and rendering, enhancing the overall user experience. Noteworthy updates were also made to `shaheedazaad/auto-zcurve` and `giovannacdelsordo/output-it-forward`, with revisions to validation notes and the addition of a Chrome Webstore link, respectively. These connections between projects demonstrate the community's collaborative spirit and commitment to advancing open-source research tools. Overall, it's been a productive period, with many improvements and updates that will benefit the community as a whole.

71 commits across 5 active repositories this week.

Dormant repos revived: escicheck

Most active:

coarse21 commits
regcheck18 commits
docpluck16 commits
metacheck13 commits
escicheck3 commits

item.title: metacheck: 6 commits today
item.description:
scienceverse/metacheck

The `dev` branch was the focus of the recent work, where a fix was made to address the "coi_check/funding_check cold-start failure" as stated in the commit message. Additionally, the `dev` branch saw the addition of `R/import-papers.R` to ensure that development installations can be completed cleanly. The `dev` branch also received updates from several merged pull requests, including `papers_databases`, `ethics_check`, and `prereg_regcheck`, which were merged as pull requests #317, #316, and #309, respectively. Another merge was made from pull request #318, which was from the `fix/code-check-paperlist` branch, also into the `dev` branch.

6 commit(s) today:

  • b8ac461 [dev] Fix coi_check/funding_check cold-start failure — Lakens
  • de41849 [dev] Add R/import-papers.R so dev installs cleanly — Lakens
  • ed8c8d3 [dev] Merge pull request #317 from scienceverse/papers_databases — Lisa DeBruine
  • e077134 [dev] Merge pull request #316 from scienceverse/ethics_check — Lisa DeBruine
  • a40d7db [dev] Merge pull request #309 from scienceverse/prereg_regcheck — Lisa DeBruine
  • 1fb2bab [dev] Merge pull request #318 from scienceverse/fix/code-check-paperlist — Lisa DeBruine
  • 8cbef44 [dev] Fix code_check crash and missing paper_id on paper lists — Lakens
  • b7afa6a [dev] add functions to load paper databases from scienceverse/papers — Lakens
  • 25c15a6 [main] update metacheck page to fix #314 — Lakens
  • 73736b0 [dev] Fix create_report_app() to report_app() in Get Started vignette — Daniel Lakens
  • 00d7953 [dev] add ethics_check + bug fix — Lakens
  • 2b8a367 [new_modules] Revert "fix app instruction #314" — Lakens
  • 1d3719b [new_modules] fix app instruction #314 — Lakens
  • dccb9f4 [main] update module names in vignettes — Lakens
  • 684ccdf [main] Update DESCRIPTION fix #313 — Daniel Lakens
  • 7d7127d [new_modules] fix #313 — Lakens
  • 9749095 [new_modules] explore ideas for z-curve and grim — Lakens
  • a7dea29 [main] Merge pull request #312 from scienceverse/dev — Lisa DeBruine
  • f09fb47 [dev] zenodo citation — Lisa DeBruine
  • 00eaa3b [main] Merge pull request #311 from scienceverse/dev — Lisa DeBruine

View all commits →

item.title: regcheck: 3 commits today
item.description:
JamieCummins/regcheck

Work was done on the 'staging' branch, where a debug of the CLI report was performed. Additionally on the 'staging' branch, further local LLM support was added in the CLI. The 'staging' branch also saw UI fixes, as well as fixes for OSF and multistudy bugs. Furthermore, on the 'staging' branch, security features were hardened.

3 commit(s) today:

  • b0d8ab2 [staging] debug of CLI report — Jamie Cummins
  • 97e8203 [staging] add further local LLM support in CLI — Jamie Cummins
  • df873ec [staging] UI fixes — Jamie Cummins
  • 5b53ad6 [staging] fix OSF + mutlistudy bugs — Jamie Cummins
  • 71be3be [staging] harden security features — Jamie Cummins
  • fbd5030 [staging] firm up qwen formatting — Jamie Cummins
  • f775856 [staging] improve qwen — Jamie Cummins
  • 724d020 [staging] re-add qwen! — Jamie Cummins
  • 89a1470 [staging] push for redeploy — Jamie Cummins
  • baebc81 [staging] hunting down osf bug — Jamie Cummins
  • 2f5eaaa [staging] improve logging — Jamie Cummins
  • 7fb80fe [staging] improve error message — Jamie Cummins
  • a2f7302 [staging] fix osf bug — Jamie Cummins
  • d2089d5 [staging] remove groq endpoints — Jamie Cummins
  • 51b9ba6 [staging] harden json handling — Jamie Cummins
  • 6cdde08 [staging] fix oss bug — Jamie Cummins
  • 1fd2de7 [staging] use more efficient storage — Jamie Cummins
  • 4b7a527 [staging] patch oss SDK handler — Jamie Cummins
  • e514303 [staging] fix oss handling bug — Jamie Cummins
  • 599489b [staging] harden qwen handling — Jamie Cummins

View all commits →

item.title: escicheck: activity update
item.description:
giladfeldman/escicheck

The `main` branch was the focus of the recent work, with two commits made to it. The first commit synced changes from `escicheckapp@bd6f007`, which corresponds to `effectcheck v0.6.5`, as stated in the commit message. The second commit, also on the `main` branch, synced changes from `escicheckapp@dec37b1`, which is associated with `effectcheck v0.6.4`. These commits brought the `main` branch up to date with versions `v0.6.4` and `v0.6.5` of `effectcheck`. The changes were made by Gilad Feldman, with the commits referenced as `#18` and `#19`.

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →
